families.label: A Matter Of Time - Seismographic Scan
families.selection_reason: Repeated shell grammar across multiple payload states makes this the best first family for reusable primitives.
families.notes: This family is the first proof that one renderer scaffold must survive more than one interior state.

families.label: The Outrageous Okona - Holodeck Selection
families.selection_reason: This family forces large elbow/header geometry, pill controls, and dense command grouping that differ materially from Seismographic.
families.notes: This is the second-family proof that the renderer can generalize beyond one sweep-centric specimen.

families.label: Rascals - Periodic Table Of Elements
families.selection_reason: This family forces dense repeated LCARS cell grammar rather than asymmetrical sweep grammar.
families.notes: Singleton family kept in first wave because it covers a primitive class the current repo does not prove.

families.label: ADGE Intro
families.selection_reason: Useful for sparse composition and typography validation, but not first-wave critical for renderer primitive extraction.
families.notes: This family is intentionally cataloged now so deferral is explicit rather than forgotten.

stable_regions.description: Large title strip with dense telemetry text remains structurally stable across the family.
Vertical numbered rail and elbow base define a fixed family scaffold.
Long horizontal bars across the center are family-defining and should not be treated as incidental decoration.

dynamic_regions.description: The lower-right content region changes between waveform, map, and analysis states.
Operational subtitle/readout copy may shift while family geometry stays fixed.

targets.acceptance_notes: Chosen to establish the first target family scaffold and to force code-rendered chart framing rather than screenshot-backed approximation.

stable_regions.description: The title strip and top telemetry density remain family-stable.
The left rail and elbow base remain fixed family structure.
The central bars remain stable while the payload mode changes.

dynamic_regions.description: This frame swaps the chart payload for a map/callout payload.
Supporting labels and local emphasis shift with the payload state.

targets.acceptance_notes: Paired with seismo_scan_a to ensure the first renderer family is state-resilient rather than frame-specific.

stable_regions.description: The large elbow/header assembly defines the family and should remain stable.
The lower frame bar assembly anchors the composition.
Left-side stacked bars and counters form a stable control grammar.

dynamic_regions.description: The central command/data grouping varies in density and content arrangement.
The right-side pill actions vary by frame.

targets.acceptance_notes: Chosen to force a second family with materially different primitive needs than Seismographic.

stable_regions.description: The large elbow/header frame remains stable across the family.
The lower frame bar assembly remains a family constant.
The left-side controls and counters remain family-stable.

dynamic_regions.description: This frame emphasizes centered list payload rather than the denser command arrangement.
Small side pills and labels vary without changing family grammar.

targets.acceptance_notes: Paired with holodeck_programming_a so the family must survive a major payload-mode change.

stable_regions.description: The title run and top frame bars are fixed structural anchors.
The lower frame bar assembly is a major family constant.
The overall matrix structure is stable and should be driven by repeated primitives.

dynamic_regions.description: This singleton target has no second-state payload pair in first-wave scope.

targets.acceptance_notes: Chosen because it proves dense repeated LCARS grammar rather than another asymmetrical sweep family.
